one_time_description: QBOW Child & Family Services Inc. reimbursement for eligible Child and Family Service prevention activity costs for period January 26, 2016 to March 31, 2019 under ISC First Nations Child and Family Services program
economic_notes: First Nation holds 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings Inc. (Swift Current Casino Development) and 4.5% interest in IMI Brokerage Limited Partnership. COVID-19 impact noted with expected negative impact on First Nations Trust and Living Sky Community Development Corporation funding due to temporary closure of SIGA casinos. Buffalo Crossing Convenience & Gas Bar operated as consolidated entity.
notes: Fiscal year 2020. Four consolidated subsidiaries: Standing Buffalo Dakota First Nation CMHC Housing, Standing Buffalo Care Home Inc., Dakota Development Corporation, and Buffalo Crossing Convenience & Gas Bar. QBOW Child & Family Services reimbursement of $588,682 relates to prior period costs (Jan 2016 - Mar 2019) and is subject to ISC review. Prior year revenue of $16.1M was significantly higher partly due to $811,002 insurance proceeds on housing and $1,733,495 from QBOW. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $8,254,906 + CMHC Subsidy/Other $420,017 + CMHC Rent $311,280 + FHQ Tribal Council $373,900 = $9,360,103. No remuneration schedule visible in extracted text. COVID-19 significant event disclosed. Net debt position of $6,596,999.
